agenda_pressure: Prefer executable recognizers over cosmetic repackaging of already verified semantics.
Favor order-independent exact reachable-region statements over ambient overapproximation machinery.
Keep decidability and grammar-facing relevance in view by prioritizing finite algorithms over one-off negatives.
current_bottlenecks: No concrete arbitrary-order fold theorem yet computes the canonical reachable-region labeling.
Intermediate reverse-sweep states still lack a stable semantic invariant.
Strict degree descent has not yet been converted into exact path-length or iteration bounds.
desired_summary_changes: An explicit fold theorem for any reverse-topological enumeration of the exact reachable region.
A reusable suffix or partial-sweep invariant identifying each intermediate state of the fold.
An exact distance-to-base characterization yielding sharp saturation-round convergence.
important_verified_counterexamples: For every Γ : List Tp and s : String, (¬ ∃ q, AtomicResidualGraphStep (Γ, s) q) ↔ ∀ B ∈ Γ, is_atom B.
For every finite list src and atom s, the class of states p such that p.2 = s and every formula B in p.1 is a subformula of some formula C in src is finite.

Prove a one-sided exactness theorem for ordinary support closure: if every formula in `Γ` and `A` is left-only, then `SupportClosure Γ A ↔ HandedSupportClosure Γ A`; dually, if every formula in `Γ` and `A` is ...
missing_bridges: A bridge from canonical labeling and least subsets to an explicit List.foldr computation.
A bridge from predecessor closure to operator-level fixed-point and iterative saturation formulations.
A bridge from strict degree descent to exact path metrics and convergence time.

next_direction.guidance: Prioritize arbitrary reverse-topological fold correctness with a reusable suffix invariant, and secondarily the exact distance and saturation theorems that turn descent into quantitative control.
next_direction.label: arbitrary_fold_and_distance_bounds
next_direction.rationale: The exact reachable DAG already has canonical labels and least-subset semantics, while verified ambient-slice and mixed-terminal counterexamples show that more boundary examples are lower value than executable computation and convergence bounds.

overexplored_patterns: Ambient bounded-slice exactness or separation questions outside the exact reachable region.
Universal-terminal or all-sinks summaries already weakened by mixed-terminal evidence.
Presentation-only restatements of existing least-set or labeling theorems without explicit computation.

canonical_targets: A deeper understanding of completeness for $L$
The boundary between cut elimination and decidability in extensions
soft_constraints: Closeness to established notation and theory
themes: Structural correspondences between language hierarchies and logical systems
Extraction of logical rules and complexity

theory_snapshot: The residual theory now has an exact reachable-region core: from any residual root, the reachable region is finite, degree-descending, and acceptance is exactly reachability of the distinguished singleton base state. On that exact DAG, acceptance already has canonical Boolean-label, least predecessor-closed subset, topological-order, and terminal-state characterizations. The frontier is to convert those abstract exact-region semantics into an explicit reverse-topological fold with a stable suffix invariant, and to extract exact distance and saturation-round bounds from the same descent structure. Verified ambient-slice infinitude and mixed-terminal counterexamples still block ambient overapproximation and universal-sink summaries as primary directions.
